summary: Matched several functions in src/melee/gm/gm_18A5.c and refined supporting game-mode types, headers, static data, and symbol splits. The PR replaces multiple m2c/UNK stubs with typed C for tournament/versus setup, camera helpers, random character selection, and scene transition helpers, while also correcting related function signatures in gm_1601, gm_1B03, and gmmain_lib. Static data around lbl_803D9D20 was split into more accurate objects such as lbl_803D9DD0 and lbl_803D9F0C, with config/GALE01/symbols.txt updated to reflect the new data boundaries.
what_changed: Implemented fn_8018F310 as a lookup over lbl_803D9D20.x59, returning the matching index or -1.
Replaced an M2C_FIELD access in fn_8018F3BC with a typed array access to lbl_803D9D20.x59[arg0].
Implemented fn_8018F3D0 as a small classifier over numeric character/icon IDs, returning 0, 1, or 2 based on explicit ranges and values.
Implemented fn_8018F410 using HSD_Randi(0x19) to choose a valid random entry from lbl_803D9D20.x72 while avoiding the three most recent choices stored in lbl_803D9F0C.
Added typed camera helpers fn_80190480, fn_801904D0, and fn_80190520 using lbl_803D9DD0 rather than treating the camera object as part of lbl_803D9D20.

Implemented gm_801905F0(StartMeleeData*) to initialize match rules and players from GameRules, gm_804771C4 TmData, item settings, handicap settings, stock/time settings, random character selection, and stage metadata.
Implemented gm_80190EA4 to set up cameras, lights, models, tournament-mode display state, special camera positioning, and a 64-entry loop initializing tmdata->x37[i].x1 according to handicap mode.
Implemented fn_80196564 and fn_80196594 as boolean state predicates over TmData fields x14 and x32, and implemented fn_801965C4 to set pending minor scenes and update tournament progression state.
Changed gm_801B1810 so it captures StartMeleeData* from gm_801A427C(arg0) and passes it to gm_801905F0(data), replacing the previous no-argument call.

Corrected nearby signatures: fn_801653E8 returns f32/float instead of f64, gm_80169238 takes s8 instead of int, gmMainLib_8015ED30 returns int instead of u8, fn_8018E618 takes int,int,float, fn_801902F0 takes int, fn_80190ABC takes int, gm_80190EA4 takes void, and fn_801965C4 returns void.

Expanded struct TmData in src/melee/gm/types.h with explicit int fields x8 through x24, clarified UnkSelections fields for slot type, color, CPU level, and a u16 field x6, and noted that x37 likely needs to be larger than 16 because gm_80190EA4 indexes 64 entries.

Reworked gm_18A5.static.h static data: made gm_804771C4 a defined TmData object, gave lbl_803D9D20 typed byte arrays x0, x59, and x72 with initializer data, split camera defaults into lbl_803D9DD0_t, added lbl_803D9DAC float data, added lbl_803D9E1C u32 data, and added lbl_803D9F0C history state initialized to -1.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to split data objects around 803D9DAC/803D9DD0/803D9E1C and 803D9F00/803D9F0C, changing object sizes to match the newly identified static data boundaries.
smart_moves: Splitting lbl_803D9D20 into typed substructures and separate data symbols made existing code simpler and eliminated raw M2C_FIELD access.
Using a small inline helper gm_801905F0_inline0 for character-kind remapping isolated a matching-sensitive transformation from the much larger gm_801905F0 implementation.
Adding #pragma dont_inline around gm_8018F634 and gm_80190EA4 indicates the author preserved compiler behavior needed for matching rather than allowing helpful-looking inlining to perturb codegen.
Correcting function signatures in callers and headers allowed matched functions to pass real typed values, especially StartMeleeData* through gm_801B1810 into gm_801905F0.
Updating symbols.txt together with static.h kept linker/data boundaries synchronized with the newly understood data layout.
The random-selection implementation tracks the last three selected IDs in lbl_803D9F0C, a useful behavioral clue for interpreting the static data and avoiding treating it as anonymous padding.
decomp_lessons: When M2C_FIELD appears against a global blob, look for symbol-boundary mistakes or missing static substructures; splitting data can unlock direct field/array accesses and cleaner matches.
Header signature fixes are often prerequisites for matching larger functions: return width changes such as f64 to f32 and u8 to int can materially affect generated code.
Do not assume a single .data symbol is one C object. This PR split contiguous data into arrays, structs, and small state globals based on use sites and symbol sizes.
A large setup function can become matchable once related leaf helpers, data layouts, and caller prototypes are corrected first.
Preserve explicit no-inline pragmas when a tiny accessor or setup function would otherwise be inlined by the compiler and break matching.

Field comments in partially known structs should reflect evidence from use sites but remain cautious; the PR uses comments such as slot type, color, CPU level, and a TODO about x37 needing a larger size.
naming_conventions: Kept unknown functions and globals under address-based names such as fn_8018F410, gm_801905F0, lbl_803D9D20, lbl_803D9DD0, and lbl_803D9F0C.
Used suffix _t for local static struct type names such as struct lbl_803D9D20_t and struct lbl_803D9DD0_t.
Used x-offset field names such as x0, x4, x8, x14, x28, x4B8, x59, and x72 for incompletely understood struct fields.
Named a local inline helper gm_801905F0_inline0, tying it to the parent function and preserving its anonymous/decomp-specific status.
Used descriptive existing project types where known, including TmData, StartMeleeData, GameRules, TmVsData, HSD_CObj, HSD_JObj, HSD_GObj, HSD_Text, and Vec3.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed fn_801653E8 to return f32/float so the damage-ratio calculation 0.1f * arg0 has the expected single-precision behavior.
Changed gmMainLib_8015ED30 from u8 to int so switch cases in gm_801905F0 can consume the value without unsigned-byte return artifacts.
Used explicit casts and comparisons such as `(int) arg8 == 0` and `(s32) x == 0` in camera helpers, likely to match integer-conversion codegen rather than pure float comparison.
Introduced temporary pointers in fn_801904D0/fn_80190520 and pointer locals temp_r30/temp_r29 in fn_8018F410, which may help produce the desired register allocation.
Used a do-while loop around HSD_Randi in fn_8018F410 to match retry-until-valid random selection control flow.
Applied #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop around gm_8018F634 and gm_80190EA4 to control compiler inlining for matching.
Replaced `gm_801905F0();` with `gm_801905F0(data);` after typing gm_801A427C as returning StartMeleeData*, aligning caller codegen and callee prototype.
Used MIN(gm_804771C4.x4B8[i].x6, 0x78), explicit loops over four players, and explicit loops over 64 tmdata entries to reflect observed bounds rather than assumed struct array sizes.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided slice. The PR was merged quickly and the human text excerpt is empty, so there is no evidence of reviewer-requested changes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further type lbl_803D9D20, lbl_803D9DD0, or lbl_803D9F0C.
Search for follow-up work expanding TmData.x37 beyond 16 entries or resolving the TODO noted in gm_80190EA4.
Search for later matches of gm_80190FE4, fn_80190ABC, fn_80196684, and other remaining gm_18A5 stubs.
Check whether gm_801905F0_inline0 was later renamed after character-kind remapping behavior became better understood.
Look for additional PRs that split .data symbols around 803D9DAC through 803D9F18 or refine the static arrays introduced here.
